Name: Lenny's Newsletter

Name: Silicon Valley Product Group (SVPG)

Name: Product Thinking

Name: Shreyas Doshi (Free Newsletter)

Name: Product Growth
Description: One of the largest paid PM newsletters. Deep dives on growth PM, AI product management, monetization, activation/retention, case studies, and landing PM roles at AI companies.

Name: The Product Compass

Name: Roman Pichler

Name: Product Talk

Name: Product Thoughts / Product Practice

Name: Bring the Donuts

Name: Rich Mironov's Product Bytes

Name: ONE THING

Name: GTM Strategist
Description: Go-to-market strategy, product-market fit, beachhead/segment selection, positioning, pricing, and launch tactics, with concrete examples and frameworks for taking products to market.

Name: The Outcome

Name: Ask Gib

Name: Ravi on Product

Name: Maarten's Newsletter

Name: Behind Product Lines

Name: The Product Person

Name: The Uncommon Executive

Name: Focused Chaos

Name: Productify

Name: Product Manager HQ

Name: PM Talk

Name: Product Management with Mani

Name: Productside Newsletter

Name: GoPractice

Name: Casey Accidental
Description: Deep essays on growth strategy, retention, growth loops, marketplaces, and scaling product orgs from the ex-CPO of Eventbrite and growth lead at Pinterest/Grubhub.

Description: Deeply researched essays on growth strategy, product-channel fit, growth loops, retention, and AI's impact on distribution from the Reforge founder and ex-VP Growth at HubSpot.

Name: The Neuron

Name: Every

Name: TheSequence

Name: Marketing Ideas

Name: Money Stuff

Name: Noahpinion
Description: Economics, technology, and policy commentary that frames macro and industry trends in plain language. Useful for building a model of how the broader economy shapes markets and products.

Name: Kyla's Newsletter
Description: Human-centric economic analysis connecting markets, sentiment, and culture (coined "vibecession"). Accessible takes on how the economy feels versus how it measures.

Name: Benedict Evans
Description: Essays on technology strategy, platform shifts, and the structural forces shaping tech, from a former a16z partner. Synthesizes market data into clear strategic frameworks—essential for reasoning about platform-level change.

Name: Doomberg

Name: Spyglass

Name: Where's Your Ed At

Name: Musings on Markets

Name: Remains of the Day

Name: kwokchain
Description: Infrequent but high-signal essays on strategy, narrative distillation, business models, and venture dynamics. Dense, conceptual reading on positioning and moats (known for the "Narrative Distillation" framework).

Name: The Information

Name: Sub Club

Description: Curated insights and stories from the smartest people in subscription apps—mobile monetization, paywalls, trials, pricing, and retention. Pairs with the Sub Club podcast and State of Subscription Apps benchmarks.

Name: Elemental Growth

Name: Ibbaka Market Blog
Description: Strategic B2B SaaS and value-based pricing insights—economic value estimation, value metrics, usage/credit-based pricing, and AI monetization. Analytical, advisory-grade pieces on pricing strategy.

Name: Subscribed (Zuora)

Name: Press Gazette

Name: A Media Operator

Name: Subscription Insider
Description: Subscription-economy news, research, and training for operators across pricing, payments, retention, and compliance. Tracks public-company subscription earnings and recurring-revenue strategy.

Name: SaaSletter

Name: GP Bullhound CSS

Name: NFX
Primary Author(s): James Currier, Gigi Levy-Weiss et al. (NFX)
Description: Essays and frameworks on network effects and marketplace defensibility from the NFX seed fund—home of the Network Effects Bible, marketplace playbooks, and B2B/consumer teardowns. Required reading for two-sided businesses.

Name: Sarah Tavel
Description: Benchmark GP and author of the canonical "Hierarchy of Marketplaces" and "Hierarchy of Engagement." Deep, practitioner essays on building winner-take-most marketplaces, liquidity, and consumer network effects.

Name: Above the Crowd
Description: The legendary Benchmark investor's long-running blog on marketplaces, online businesses, unit economics, take rates, and platform dynamics. Infrequent but foundational essays.

Name: Platforms (Sangeet Paul Choudary)
Description: Author of Platform Revolution on platform strategy, ecosystem competition, platformization, and how AI reshapes platform power and the knowledge economy. Rigorous, framework-driven analysis.

Name: Newcomer
Description: Deeply reported newsletter on the startup/VC industry—fundraising scoops, power dynamics inside venture firms, and interviews. Strong on marketplace, consumer, and platform-company financings and strategy.

Name: 2PM
Description: Where media meets commerce: curated news plus original analysis on DTC, retail, brand, and the commerce-media intersection. A cult favorite among commerce operators and investors.

Name: Modern Retail
Description: Original journalism on the reinvention of retail—DTC brand evolution, the Amazon era, retail media networks, marketplaces, and omnichannel. Deeper investigative pieces than most daily retail briefings.

Name: Practical Ecommerce
Description: Independent how-to journalism for online merchants—Amazon, SEO, conversion, payments, multichannel, and shipping. Hands-on, operator-focused content for running an ecommerce business.

Name: The Consumer VC
Description: Newsletter and podcast on investing in consumer startups—marketplaces, CPG, commerce, DTC—with weekly fundraising/M&A roundups and interviews with consumer-focused VCs and founders.

Name: TSOH Investment Research
Description: Transparent long-term equity research with full portfolio disclosure, heavy on consumer, retail, and platform businesses (dollar stores, Amazon, media). Investor-grade reasoning on commerce companies.

Name: All4Marketplaces
Description: News, tools, and tactics on Amazon and other marketplaces—Seller/Vendor strategy, catalog management, and third-party selling. Practitioner-oriented coverage of the marketplace-seller ecosystem.

Name: API Evangelist
Description: The definitive long-running resource on the technology, business, politics, and people of APIs—API governance, the API economy, and platform/ecosystem strategy. Essential for PMs working on APIs and developer platforms.

Name: DTC Newsletter
Description: Weekly Sunday tactical newsletter on DTC branding, conversion, creative, and growth from a leading DTC operator/investor. Practical playbooks for launching and scaling consumer brands online.

Name: Eugene Yan
Description: Applied ML, recommender systems, and LLM systems from a Member of Technical Staff at Anthropic (ex-Amazon/Alibaba). Deep, practitioner-grade essays on building ML/recsys/LLM systems in production, plus career advice.

Name: Ahead of AI
Description: Technical deep dives into LLM architectures, curated research-paper roundups, and annual state-of-the-art reviews from the author of Build a Large Language Model From Scratch. Code-driven, implementation-focused.

Name: Chip Huyen's Blog
Description: Practitioner essays on ML systems and AI engineering in production from the author of Designing Machine Learning Systems and AI Engineering. Covers ML platform architecture, agents, and real-world deployment.

Name: The AiEdge
Description: A continuous-learning newsletter on ML applications, ML system design, MLOps, recommender systems, RAG, and agents from a former Meta ML tech lead. Serialized deep dives and hands-on tutorials.

Name: SwirlAI Newsletter
Description: Explains data engineering, ML, MLOps, and GenAI systems engineering (RAG, agentic AI, evals) in digestible form with strong visual diagrams. Connects the dots across the end-to-end data and AI pipeline.

Name: Deep (Learning) Focus
Description: In-depth research overviews on deep learning and AI from a Netflix senior research scientist. Each series focuses on one topic, distilling multiple papers with extensive background for comprehensive understanding.

Name: Hamel's Blog
Description: Focused on bringing rigorous data science to AI through "evals"—helping teams debug, analyze, and measure LLM systems. Covers error analysis, data flywheels, and trustworthy automated evaluation.

Name: Normcore Tech
Description: Thoughtful technical writing at the intersection of ML systems, engineering craft, and tech culture. Covers embeddings, LLMs, recsys-adjacent topics, and production systems, grounded in nuance.

Name: Data Engineering Weekly
Description: Weekly curated digest of the most important data-engineering writing—modern data platforms, large-scale pipelines, lakehouses, and increasingly AI-driven and ML data architectures. A staple for data/ML platform practitioners.

Name: Gradient Flow
Description: Frameworks and analysis on applied AI, small/large language models, data engineering, and AI knowledge work, from the former O'Reilly Chief Data Scientist who hosts the Data Exchange podcast.

Name: NLP News
Description: Regular analyses of advances in NLP and ML from a leading NLP researcher—contextualizing new trends, summarizing research, and offering guidance on future directions at a practitioner level.

Name: Top Information Retrieval Papers of the Week
Description: Weekly curation of the 7-10 most notable research papers in information retrieval—recommender systems, search, retrieval, and ranking—each with a brief summary. Tracks industrial work from Meta, Uber, LinkedIn.

Name: RecsysML + LLMs
Description: State-of-the-art applied ML focused on recommender systems and their convergence with LLMs—generative retrieval, RL-aligned ranking, embeddings, and ML design interviews. By a 20-year applied-ML veteran.

Name: Language Models & Co.
Description: Visual, intuitive explanations of large language models—their internals and applications—from the creator of The Illustrated Transformer and co-author of Hands-On Large Language Models.

Name: Lil'Log
Description: Rigorous, long-form learning notes on AI/ML research—LLMs, agents, reinforcement learning, diffusion, transformers—by a prominent ML researcher (ex-OpenAI). Each post is an authoritative, deeply referenced survey.

Name: MLOps Newsletter
Description: Mostly-weekly roundup of developments across machine learning and deep learning, with a strong systems/recommendations bent. Practical signal on MLOps tooling, papers, and industry engineering.

Name: Marvelous MLOps
Description: Pragmatic MLOps content focused on connecting existing tools to support real MLOps standards in production. Hands-on guidance for ML platform builders, plus an accompanying podcast.

Name: Exploring Language Models
Description: Visual guides and deep dives into large language models—quantization, mixture-of-experts, embeddings, and architecture internals—by an ML engineer and co-author of Hands-On Large Language Models.

Name: Daily Dose of Data Science
Description: Bite-sized daily lessons on data science and ML—lesser-known techniques, ML system design, MLOps, RL, LLMs, and AI agents—each readable in a couple of minutes with practical, hands-on framing.

Name: amatria.in
Description: Clear, real-world-grounded writing on AI, LLMs, reinforcement learning, and recommender systems from a leading practitioner (ex-Netflix recommendations, ex-Google AI, Curai founder). Includes recsys conference recaps.

Name: Below the Line
Description: Breaks down business models and how technology reshapes them, with deep e-commerce and marketplace economics coverage (Amazon, Shopify, Etsy, eBay). Operator-grade analysis of liquidity, network effects, and AI's P&L impact.

Name: Thought Sparks
Description: A Columbia Business School professor's essays on strategy, innovation, and growth—including inflection points and platform/ecosystem economics. Bridges academic strategy frameworks with practical company examples.

Name: Run the Business
Description: Product-leadership essays on strategy, execution, metrics, and B2B SaaS from a seasoned VP of Product. Covers north-star metrics, marketplace/platform PM, org dynamics, and durable value creation.

Name: The Secret CFO
Description: Finance-leadership wisdom not taught in textbooks, written by an anonymous veteran CFO. Covers cash flow, profitability, capital allocation, and investor relations in plain language for founders and operators.

Name: Looking for Leverage
Description: A weekly playbook for PE-backed CFOs and executives focused on margin expansion, value creation, and exit strategy. Tactical operator content on running profitable, leverageable businesses.

Name: OnlyCFO's Newsletter
Description: Financial metrics, benchmarks, and operational breakdowns for tech companies from an anonymous finance operator. Covers SaaS efficiency, AI-native finance, and capital-markets reads—sharp, numbers-driven takes.

Name: Clouded Judgement
Description: Weekly data-driven analysis of public SaaS and cloud companies from an Altimeter investor, tracking multiples, growth, efficiency, and macro signals. The go-to for SaaS valuation and capital-markets context.

Name: ByteByteGo
Description: Explains complex system design and modern web/data architecture in simple terms with strong visuals, for 1M+ readers. Covers scalability, distributed systems, and how big tech builds infrastructure—a clear primer for technical intuition.

Name: MKT1 Newsletter
Description: B2B marketing strategy, positioning, demand generation, and marketing-org design from former heads of marketing at Asana and Carta. Practical, template-rich frameworks for early-stage founders building GTM.

Name: The Down Round
Description: Candid essays on financing, scaling, and the realities of early-stage B2B software from a venture investor. Covers fundraising dynamics, valuations, and founder-VC relationships—an insider's perspective on startup capital.

Name: Wildfire Labs
Description: Founder-operator essays on building and investing in startups, venture studios, and company-building from a serial CEO. Practical lessons on operating, scaling, and capital.

Name: Innovation Unpacked (JTBD)
Description: Applies Jobs-to-be-Done thinking to innovation, product strategy, and growth—helping founders frame problems and markets. Deep on JTBD method and customer-centric strategy.

Name: Investing in AI
Description: Twice-monthly AI market analysis and investing perspective from an operator who has built and funded multiple companies. Connects AI research and product trends to business and investment implications.

Name: MRR Unlocked
Description: Actionable tactics for scaling B2B SaaS revenue, covering growth, monetization, and go-to-market experiments. Concise, practical playbooks for early-stage founders on the path to recurring revenue.

Name: Micro SaaS Idea
Description: Surfaces and validates profitable micro-SaaS business ideas, with market analysis and build-vs-buy reasoning. Idea-generation fuel for indie founders and operators—niche, opportunity-spotting focus.
